Based on 10 reviews

Review CodeRabbit?

Reviewers say CodeRabbit is most useful for speeding up pull request reviews while making human reviewers’ jobs easier. Users repeatedly praise its context-aware, line-by-line suggestions, saying it catches copy-paste mistakes, missed logic issues, and loopholes that can slip past manual review. The main limitation mentioned is a desire for broader language support. Founder feedback is also strongly positive: the makers of Hackmamba say it influenced their own product development and that they use it internally for code reviews.

results.title: Figr AI: Context-aware AI that thinks through UX. | Product Hunt
results.content:
4.7

Based on 6 reviews

Review Figr AI?

Figr is praised for its ability to streamline the design process, allowing users to create design systems quickly without sacrificing consistency. Users appreciate its unique features and the time-saving benefits it offers, making it particularly useful for both beginners and experienced designers. The tool is noted for its sleek and fluid interface, which simplifies UI/UX design. While some users suggest improvements like adding autolayout features, the overall feedback highlights Figr as an innovative and impactful tool for design and development teams.

Just tried it with the Product Hunt design file - really cool stuff. Seems like it will be very useful in quickly making realistic preliminary wireframes using design patterns and UI elements pulled from other popular apps. I can see myself Frankenstein-ing an app design using components from many different popular apps. Ex: "I want an app that has a home page feed like Product Hunt with posts like Instagram but text editing like Notion and comment threads like Twitter" and being able to quickly pull - and adjust - those components to mesh together. Gonna keep playing with the free version for now but looks good so far!

context aware (3)capture tool (8)responsive developer team (2)

Report

 14 views

•4 reviews [...] Based on 28 reviews

Review Lazy?

Reviewers mostly praise Lazy for fast, low-friction capture that helps them stay in flow while researching, writing, studying, and saving material from across the desktop. Several say it is easy to start with, useful in daily work, and especially strong at clipping context like links, transcripts, and related metadata. But the feedback is not uniformly positive: a few reviewers say access has been stuck behind a long waitlist, progress feels slow, support and billing were hard to reach, and mobile access remains limited for reading notes.

results.title: superwhisper Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
1. Simulate Keypress – Smarter Typing Automation The current Simulate Keypress feature is powerful, but with just a few tweaks, it could be even more effective: • Custom delay control between keypresses • Option to randomize keypress delays for more human-like typing • Ability to paste plain text that SuperWhisper then types out character by character (like an autotyper) These changes would be great for accessibility, automation, and productivity workflows. ⸻ 2. AI Assistant Mode – Context-Aware Voice Actions Imagine a mode where SuperWhisper becomes a lightweight AI voice assistant that: • Listens for spoken prompts or intent • Automatically executes contextual actions, like launching apps, typing commands, or navigating interfaces Even better: let it interact directly with onscreen [...] Report

 84 views

•4 reviews

Literally use it daily to communicate with AI. So helpful for a world currently where boundaries of capability varies across different frontier models depending on the problem you have in front of you. This tool completely opened up a brand new way of me being able to interact with AI - effectively allowing for me to talk 10 minutes to it at a time in one input, and having the AI have all the context it needs to either 1. help me with the problem/situation at hand, 2. reflect back to me what is it I'm missing or 3. provide value to me simply as a means of organizing and refining my thoughts via speaking. Absolutely love it and the UI is my biggest inspiration right now across all the products I've grown to use and love the past few months. Super sleek. [...] #### What's great

multi-language support (3)daily use (9)voice to text accuracy (8)customizable modes (5)

Report

 16 views

•1 review

SuperWhisper has been a game changer for me. I'm able to move much faster as I work by using my voice instead of typing. I've also discovered that I process concepts better when I verbalize them. Dictating my communications with SuperWhisper has allowed me to do that in ways that I couldn't when I was typing more. The AI integration completely negates the feeling I used to have when using traditional dictation tools (ahem, Siri). I don't have to worry about getting everything said perfectly, nor even worry about having perfect grammar. Neil, the developer, is very responsive, open to suggestions, and welcomes critical feedback.

#### What's great
